summary: Draft PR adding decompilation work for lb_80019628, lbMemory_80015320, and lbMemory_8001564C, plus a small fn_80015184 matching improvement in the melee lb library area. Bot output reported one new match for fn_80015184 and improvements for the three newly added functions, but also broken matches and regressions, and the PR closed unmerged with no human review comments in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Adds lb_80019628, which updates timing state, PAD sampling rate, and periodic alarm setup using lb_804329F0.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot reported lb_80019628 improved from 0.00% to 50.52%

key_files.role: Adds lbMemory_80015320 and lbMemory_8001564C, moves LBMgr definition earlier for use by lbMemory_80015320, adds AR/devcom includes, and changes fn_80015184 alarm setup from a raw tick constant/NULL handler to OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3)/fn_80015184.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added void lbMemory_80015320(s32 arg0, Handle* arg1, void* arg2, s32 cancelflag)
raw_diff: OSSetAlarm(&temp_r3->alarm, 0x10624DD3, NULL) changed to OSSetAlarm(&temp_r3->alarm, OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3), fn_80015184)
text_corpus: bot reported fn_80015184 new match at 100.00%, lbMemory_8001564C improved to 93.52%, lbMemory_80015320 improved to 91.00%

key_files.role: Adds public prototype for lbMemory_80015320 with recovered parameter types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added /* 015320 */ void lbMemory_80015320(s32, Handle*, void*, s32);

what_changed.text: Added lb_80019628 in lb_0192.c. The function compares lb_804329F0 timing fields, clamps a negative slot field to 0, derives ticks from OS_TIMER_CLOCK or stored timing values, caps PAD sampling rate at 11, updates PADSetSamplingRate, and recreates/sets a periodic OS alarm with lb_80019900 as handler.

Added lbMemory_80015320 in lbmemory.c. The function walks Handle records, updates g_alloc.x6E4, uses HSD_DevComRequest for addresses below 0x80000000, otherwise stages a transfer through an LBMgr stored at g_alloc+0x6A0 with OS interrupt protection and a short alarm.
Changed fn_80015184 alarm requeue to use OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) and fn_80015184 instead of raw constant 0x10624DD3 and NULL.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot reported fn_80015184 as 100.00% new match

what_changed.text: Added lbMemory_8001564C in lbmemory.c. It initializes AR memory bounds, builds two Handle free lists using for loops over 0xC-byte Handle slots, and initializes g_alloc.x69C with lbMemory_80014E24.
Updated lbmemory.h with a non-static prototype for lbMemory_80015320.

smart_moves.text: Promoted a recovered lbMemory_80015320 signature to the owning header instead of keeping only a source-local static declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed static forward declaration in lbmemory.c
raw_diff: added lbMemory_80015320 prototype in lbmemory.h

smart_moves.text: Replaced a magic alarm tick value with OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3), and the bot report attributes a new 100% match for fn_80015184 after the change.
Used natural for loops to initialize repeated Handle free-list links in lbMemory_8001564C rather than writing out repeated pointer assignments.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMemory_8001564C for loops over i < 0x81 and i < 7

smart_moves.text: Added SDK/library includes for recovered APIs used by the new bodies instead of relying on implicit declarations.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched sibling or callback function contains a raw timing constant, check whether an SDK macro such as OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS expresses the original source. In this slice, changing 0x10624DD3 to OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) alongside the alarm handler change corresponded with fn_80015184 becoming a bot-reported 100% match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report, fn_80015184 new match

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated initialization of fixed-size Handle pools, natural for loops over the slot stride can be plausible decomp source and align with the global natural-loops standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMemory_8001564C loops over g_alloc.x8 and g_alloc.x638

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a recovered function body proves a callable signature that other files or callbacks may need, move the declaration to the owning header rather than leaving a stale static declaration.

naming_conventions.convention: Unknown or only address-identified functions keep address-style names such as lb_80019628 and lbMemory_80015320.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: names listed as lb_80019628, lbMemory_80015320, lbMemory_8001564C
raw_diff: function definitions use address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: Partially understood struct fields retain offset-style names such as x0_arenaLo, x4_arenaHi, x6E4, x6E8, and lb_804329F0.x40 when semantics are not fully proven by the PR evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct Allocator and lb_80019628 field accesses

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary compiler-register-style local names remained in lbMemory_80015320, indicating the slice did not fully semantic-name that function's locals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: temp_r30, temp_r28, temp_r3, temp_r26, temp_r29, temp_r31, temp_r25

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: SDK macro substitution for alarm interval: OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) replaced the raw constant 0x10624DD3 in fn_80015184.
Callback handler correction or hypothesis: OSSetAlarm in fn_80015184 was changed from NULL to fn_80015184.
Temporary internal struct recovery: LBMgr was defined with OSAlarm and transfer fields, then used for g_alloc+0x6A0 alarm/devcom state.

Raw pointer and offset casts remain in allocator recovery, especially for g_alloc.x8/g_alloc.x638 Handle pools and g_alloc+0x6A0 LBMgr access; these look like bridge code rather than fully typed allocator layout.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMemory_8001564C Handle casts over g_alloc.x8 and g_alloc.x638

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Bot report used as narrow matching signal: fn_80015184 reached 100%, while added functions remained partial and some existing items regressed.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 66.98% with -128 bytes overall. It reported 1 new match: main/melee/lb/lbmemory fn_80015184 from 88.61% to 100.00%. It reported improvements for lbMemory_8001564C to 93.52%, lbMemory_80015320 to 91.00%, and lb_80019628 to 50.52%. It also reported broken matches for lbMemory_80014E24 and lbMemory_800154D4 and regressions for lbmemory .data and lbMemory_80014FC8.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.
evidence_refs: loaded_files: text_corpus contains PR body and one bot issue comment

follow_up_queries: lbMemory_8001564C ARAlloc ARFree ARGetSize free list
lbmemory broken matches lbMemory_80014E24 lbMemory_800154D4 lbMemory_80014FC8 .data

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2490 state CLOSED, merged_at null, draft true in raw_pr_json
human_pr_text: PR body describes added missing functions and fn_80015184 improvement
notes: Diff evidence is complete for the loaded slice and supports the changed-files and code-shape summary.
Bot report provides matching evidence, including one 100% function match and several partial improvements.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so this intake should not treat these changes as merged or accepted project history.
No human review feedback is available; reviewer intent and acceptance cannot be inferred.
Added functions remained partial according to bot output: lbMemory_8001564C 93.52%, lbMemory_80015320 91.00%, lb_80019628 50.52%.
Bot report also showed regressions and broken matches, so tactics from this PR should be curated cautiously.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use SDK timer macros when they explain raw alarm constants
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2490, fn_80015184 changed OSSetAlarm from raw constant 0x10624DD3 with NULL handler to OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) with fn_80015184 as handler; the bot report marked fn_80015184 as a new 100% match. Candidate lesson: for OSAlarm code, test SDK time macros before preserving magic constants.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered non-static lb function signatures belong in lbmemory.h
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2490 removed the source-local static declaration for lbMemory_80015320 and added a typed prototype to src/melee/lb/lbmemory.h after adding the body. This supports the existing truthful-headers practice for recovered signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added lbMemory_80015320 prototype in lbmemory.h

source_update_candidates.title: lbmemory.c allocator code touches AR, devcom, and OSAlarm APIs
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2490 evidence shows src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c using ARAlloc/ARFree/ARGetSize, HSD_DevComRequest, OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, OSCreateAlarm, OSSetAlarm, and OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS while recovering lbMemory_80015320, fn_80015184, and lbMemory_8001564C.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped fact for future lbmemory decomp work; should be curated as path context, not a global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: lb_0192.c lb_80019628 controls PAD sampling rate and periodic alarm timing
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2490 added lb_80019628 in src/melee/lb/lb_0192.c. The function updates lb_804329F0 timing fields, computes a PAD sampling rate capped at 11, calls PADSetSamplingRate, and manages an OS periodic alarm with lb_80019900 as handler.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path-specific behavioral context; evidence is from an unmerged draft PR and should be verified against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote lb_80019628, lbMemory_80015320, or lbMemory_8001564C as matched; bot output reports only 50.52%, 91.00%, and 93.52% respectively.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or requested changes; the loaded slice has no human review comments.
Do not treat this PR as merged or accepted source history; metadata shows state CLOSED and merged_at null.
Do not generalize raw pointer casts in lbMemory_80015320/lbMemory_8001564C as preferred style; current standards prefer typed fields over pointer math, and this PR left bridge-like casts in place.
Do not ignore the bot-reported broken matches and regressions when reusing tactics from this PR.
